title: azolla filiculoides subsp. cristata
intro: Azolla filiculoides subsp. cristata, also known as the red water fern, is a subspecies of the floating fern Azolla filiculoides, which is a type of aquatic plant commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Azolla filiculoides subsp. cristata include the potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and support liver function. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or extract, and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine practices.
